2012-10-01.title: The Goofs and Gaffes in Presidential Debates
2012-10-01.abstract: A video archive of the most memorable moments in U.S. presidential debates wouldn’t feature many exchanges over taxes, schools or national security. It would be more like a blooper reel.

2012-10-01.title: U.S. May Have Put Mistaken Faith in Libya Site’s Security
2012-10-01.abstract: Defenses at the American mission in Benghazi have become a major issue as the Obama administration struggles to explain what happened there and how Ambassador J. Christopher Stevens ended up alone.

2012-10-01.title: Plan for Charter City to Fight Honduras Poverty Loses Its Initiator
2012-10-01.abstract: Paul Romer, an expert on economic growth, is out of his own project, tripped up by the sort of opaque decision making that his plan was supposed to change.

2012-10-01.title: 2 Supporters of a Challenger Are Killed as Venezuela’s Election Nears
2012-10-01.abstract: Gunfire erupted at a confrontation between supporters of President Hugo Chávez and his main rival, Henrique Capriles Radonski.

2012-10-01.title: Fearing Rigged Vote, Georgians Prepare for Elections
2012-10-01.abstract: The leader of the country’s Orthodox Church has warned against vote fraud by the government, and opposition voters are preparing to take to the streets.

2012-10-02.title: Lean Times in Greece as Government Cuts More Spending
2012-10-02.abstract: The Greek government presented its 2013 budget to Parliament on Monday, which contained millions of Euros in spending cuts.

2012-10-02.title: A New Sign of Distress as Iran’s Currency Falls
2012-10-02.abstract: The rial has fallen in value by about 40 percent over the past week, battered by economic sanctions and new anxieties among Iranians about their government.

2012-10-02.title: U.S. Abandoning Hopes for Taliban Peace Deal
2012-10-02.abstract: Once ambitious American plans for ending the war in Afghanistan are now being replaced by the far more modest goal of setting the stage for Afghans to work out a deal among themselves.

2012-10-02.title: Swiss Cows Send Texts to Announce They’re in Heat
2012-10-02.abstract: In Switzerland, where cows are under stress to be more productive and are showing fewer signs of being in heat, a new device lets farmers know when the time is right.

2012-10-02.title: Fighting and Chaos Spread Through Syrian City, as Services Vanish
2012-10-02.abstract: In a striking contrast from six weeks ago, battles in Syria’s largest city, Aleppo, have spread into areas that had been sheltered, threatening to open new rifts among ethnic groups.

2012-10-03.title: The Road to the Schoolhouse Proves Perilous for India’s Young
2012-10-03.abstract: India leads the world in total traffic fatalities, and children on their way to and from school make up a surprising number of them.

2012-10-03.title: In New Era, Egyptian Police Mimic the Old, Critics Say
2012-10-03.abstract: Two deaths at a police station last month have stoked fears that Egypt is not moving beyond its repressive past.

2012-10-03.title: France: Strauss-Kahn Investigation Dropped
2012-10-03.abstract: Prosecutors in Lille have dropped a sexual assault investigation into Dominique Strauss-Kahn after a Belgian escort recanted her original account about a brutal encounter.

2012-10-03.title: Japan: Whaling Group Suffers Financial Loss
2012-10-03.abstract: The government group that operates Japan’s widely criticized research whaling suffered a nearly crippling financial blow last year when its annual hunt was disrupted by an environmental group.

2012-10-03.title: Alarm Grows in São Paulo as More Police Officers Are Murdered
2012-10-03.abstract: The increase in murders of police officers, up almost 40 percent in a year, has led to fears that a powerful criminal organization is restarting a war of retaliation.

2012-10-04.title: In Organic-Hungry Hong Kong, Corn as High as an Elevator’s Climb
2012-10-04.abstract: As millions of residents worry about produce from mainland China, more are tending tiny plots on rooftops, on balconies and in far-flung corners of the city.

2012-10-04.title: China Jails 4 Tibetans Linked to Self-Immolation Protests
2012-10-04.abstract: Two of the Tibetans, teenage Buddhist monks, were charged with helping a fellow monk burn himself to death, and two were charged with leaking news of the protests.

2012-10-04.title: Despite Predictions, Jewish Homeland in Siberia Retains Its Appeal
2012-10-04.abstract: Birobidzhan, the capital of Russia’s Jewish Autonomous Region, never became the agrarian, socialist utopia that some founders envisioned, but it remains a Jewish place.

2012-10-04.title: Cajoling, Drugging and More as Rebels Try to Draw Defectors
2012-10-04.abstract: Without defections, the Syrian rebels say the opposition cannot hope to grow, never mind prevail in its battle with the government of President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-10-04.title: Up on the Roof, a Real-Life Farmville
2012-10-04.abstract: Rooftop farms in some of the world’s most crowded cities, including Hong Kong, New York and Tokyo, are adding a bit of green to the gray. They are reconnecting city dwellers with nature, teaching consumers about homegrown food and offering a glimpse, perhaps, of a more secure and sustainable food supply.

2012-10-05.title: F.B.I. Agents Scour Ruins of Attacked U.S. Diplomatic Compound in Libya
2012-10-05.abstract: Security fears had kept the agents from traveling from the American Embassy in Tripoli, the Libyan capital, to collect evidence in Benghazi.

2012-10-05.title: Karzai Accuses U.S. of Duplicity in Fighting Afghan Enemies
2012-10-05.abstract: The Afghan president, Hamid Karzai, said the United States was playing a “double game” by not taking the fight against insurgents to Pakistan and by not supplying his country with weapons.

2012-10-05.title: Winning Coalition in Georgia Demands Vote Recounts
2012-10-05.abstract: The country’s fragile postelection calm was shaken Thursday, with some coalition members calling for punishing officials from the departing government.

2012-10-05.title: F.B.I. Says Russians Smuggled Out U.S. Microchips
2012-10-05.abstract: The scheme described by law enforcement focused on microchips and other electronic components that circulate freely in the United States market, but still require a license to export.

2012-10-05.title: Iran Offers Plan, Dismissed by U.S., on Nuclear Crisis
2012-10-05.abstract: With sanctions contributing to major protests in Iran, officials have drawn up a plan to defuse the nuclear crisis with the West, but it requires many concessions.

2012-10-06.title: In Myanmar, Identity Is Challenged
2012-10-06.abstract: After nearly five decades of military rule in the country, the arrival of democracy last year has led to debates about national identity.

2012-10-06.title: Catalan Leader Boldly Grasps a Separatist Lever
2012-10-06.abstract: Artur Mas, Catalonia’s leader, says he is serious about letting Catalans decide whether to secede, and this posturing has thrust him to the forefront of Spanish politics.

2012-10-06.title: New Clashes at Site in Jerusalem Holy to Both Muslims and Jews
2012-10-06.abstract: Two Israeli police officers were slightly injured during a clash at a holy site in Jerusalem that erupted during a demonstration after Friday Prayer.

2012-10-06.title: Defiant Message Amid Iran Currency Crisis
2012-10-06.abstract: At Friday Prayer, clerics accused the United States of leading an economic war against Iran, and asserted that the currency crisis would ease.

2012-10-06.title: Cuba: Blogger Detained as Trial Begins
2012-10-06.abstract: The Cuban authorities detained the dissident blogger Yoani Sánchez for 30 hours before releasing her late Friday.

2012-10-07.title: Shlomo Venezia Dies at 88; Wrote of Auschwitz Horror
2012-10-07.abstract: For nearly 50 years, Mr. Venezia said little about his concentration camp experiences. That changed when he saw right-wing extremism re-emerge in Italy.

2012-10-07.title: Western Peace Activists Protest in Pakistan Against Drone Strikes
2012-10-07.abstract: American peace activists, many of them from the group Codepink, are among those in a Pakistani convoy trying to reach the tribal area of South Waziristan. They are protesting the U.S. campaign of drone strikes against Taliban fighters and other militants, attacks that a senior American diplomat acknowledged have caused civilian deaths.

2012-10-07.title: U.S. Agrees to Let South Korea Extend Range of Ballistic Missiles
2012-10-07.abstract: The agreement was part of a pact that tries to balance fears in the United States of a regional arms race and South Korea’s concern over military threats from North Korea.

2012-10-07.title: Philippine Rebel Group Agrees to Peace Accord to End Violence in South
2012-10-07.abstract: The deal with the Moro Islamic Liberation Front, which has fought a war of independence for more than three decades, is expected to reduce the violence in the predominantly Muslim southern island of Mindanao.

2012-10-07.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-10-07.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-10-08.title: In Pakistan, Drone Protest Takes Detour for Safety
2012-10-08.abstract: A demonstration against American drone strikes inside Pakistan’s tribal belt was abandoned after the military warned of “imminent danger,” but thousands rallied at a safer location.

2012-10-08.title: Lebanon Says Israeli Planes Circled Its Airspace for an Hour
2012-10-08.abstract: The morning after the Israeli Air Force shot down an unidentified drone, the Lebanese government said that four Israeli warplanes spent an hour illegally circling in its airspace.

2012-10-08.title: Happy Birthday, Mr. President: Putin Turns 60, and Russians Pay Notice
2012-10-08.abstract: For leaders of the Kremlin, turning 60 is a milestone heavy with history and symbolism, sometimes marked by elaborate ceremony and sometimes not.

2012-10-08.title: Vettel Wins Second Straight, and Kobayashi Shines at Home
2012-10-08.abstract: Fernando Alonso of Ferrari crashed out of the Japanese Grand Prix and his main challengers all scored points, leaving the winner, Sebastian Vettel of Red Bull, just four points behind Alonso in the driver championships. But the star of the day was the Japanese driver Kamui Kobayashi, who finished third in his Sauber.

2012-10-08.title: Ye Olde Partisanship Is Nothing New
2012-10-08.abstract: Think the partisanship in the American political system is bad now? A generation ago it was even more pronounced and pessimistic.

2012-10-09.title: Radicalism Prompts Warnings in France
2012-10-09.abstract: News of police raids in France aimed at young extremist Muslims added to the anxieties of French Jews and Muslims worried about the expansion of radical Islam.

2012-10-09.title: Mexico Gang Leader Held in Massacre of Migrants
2012-10-09.abstract: The Mexican authorities say Salvador Alfonso Martínez Escobedo planned the killings of dozens of Central and South Americans hauled from buses and trucks heading north.

2012-10-09.title: South Africa Has a Black ‘Idol.’ The Surprise Is That It’s a First.
2012-10-09.abstract: In South Africa, nothing, not even a singing competition, escapes examination under a powerful racial lens.

2012-10-09.title: Two Decades After Apartheid, a Black ‘Idol’
2012-10-09.abstract: After eight seasons, “Idols SA,” the South African version of “American Idol,” has crowned its first black pop idol — one who represents 80 percent of the country’s population. Here, a look at all the winners.

2012-10-09.title: Chávez Calls for Unity After Victory in Venezuela
2012-10-09.abstract: But President Hugo Chávez appeared unlikely to grant concessions to the opposition, despite its best showing against him.

2012-10-10.title: Italy: Berlusconi Will Not Seek Re-election as Premier
2012-10-10.abstract: Former Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi of Italy said Tuesday that he would not seek re-election next spring in order to regroup the center-right and pose a viable challenge to the center-left.

2012-10-10.title: Chile: Bishop Accused of Sexually Abusing a Minor Quits
2012-10-10.abstract: The Vatican on Tuesday accepted the resignation of a Chilean bishop, Marco Antonio Ordenes Fernandez, accused of sexually abusing a minor.

2012-10-10.title: The Hague: Libya Claims Right to Try Qaddafi’s Son and In-Law
2012-10-10.abstract: The International Criminal Court in The Hague held its first public hearing on Tuesday in the dispute on where to try Seif al-Islam el-Qaddafi and his brother-in-law Abdullah Senussi.

2012-10-10.title: A Mayor With a Prime Minister in His Shadow
2012-10-10.abstract: Mayor Boris Johnson of London, the crazy-haired and incorrigible provocateur, is often a thorn in the side of his fellow Conservative Party member, Prime Minister David Cameron.

2012-10-10.title: Brazilian Corruption Case Raises Hopes for Judicial System
2012-10-10.abstract: In a country where elite citizens are often shielded by the courts, a scandal where prominent politicians and bankers may be punished has caught the public’s imagination in Brazil.

2012-10-11.title: Smashed Skull Serves as Grim Symbol of Seething Patriotism
2012-10-11.abstract: Li Jianli, who was attacked in Xi’an, China, for driving a Japanese car, has become a symbol of what can go wrong when latent nationalism over disputed territories spins out of control.

2012-10-11.title: West Is Foolish to Celebrate Iran’s Rial Crisis, Ayatollah Says
2012-10-11.abstract: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, dismissed last week’s protest over the country’s plummeting currency as an anomaly that the West had misinterpreted as a harbinger of crisis.

2012-10-11.title: At Hague, Libya Insists It Should Try Qaddafi Son
2012-10-11.abstract: Libyan authorities argued Wednesday at the International Criminal Court at The Hague that Seif al-Islam el-Qaddafi and the former intelligence chief of Libya must be tried in their own country.

2012-10-11.title: Peru: Fujimori Family Requests Pardon
2012-10-11.abstract: The children of former President Alberto Fujimori of Peru, who is serving a prison sentence for human rights violations and corruption, requested on Wednesday for a pardon for their father.

2012-10-11.title: Afghan Officials Denounce Western Group’s Report on Country’s Future
2012-10-11.abstract: Officials in Afghanistan blasted Western news media and research organizations as political agents after the release of a report that suggests the possibility of the country’s government collapsing.

2012-10-12.title: Belfast Clinic for Abortions Due to Open
2012-10-12.abstract: A center in Belfast is expected to draw women from across Ireland, where abortion remains highly restricted.

2012-10-12.title: The West’s Stalwart Ally in the War on Drugs: Iran (Yes, That Iran)
2012-10-12.abstract: Iranian forces are seizing the highest amounts of opiates and heroin worldwide, a United Nations report says.

2012-10-12.title: Hezbollah Says It Flew Iranian-Designed Drone Into Israel
2012-10-12.abstract: The Lebanese militant group said the drone, which was shot down by Israeli forces, had been designed in Iran and assembled by Hezbollah experts in Lebanon.

2012-10-12.title:
Panetta Warns of
Dire Threat of
Cyberattack on U.S.
2012-10-12.abstract: Defense Secretary Leon E. Panetta warned that the United States was facing the possibility of a “cyber-Pearl Harbor” and was increasingly vulnerable to foreign computer hackers.

2012-10-12.title: White House Appoints Veteran Retired Diplomat to Serve as Senior Envoy in Libya
2012-10-12.abstract: The Obama administration recalled Laurence Pope, who retired from the Foreign Service 12 years ago, to serve as the senior American envoy in Libya.

2012-10-13.title: Russia Protests Treatment of Russians Arrested in Texas
2012-10-13.abstract: Several Russians accused of illegally exporting microelectronics to Russia have been subjected to “psychological and moral pressure” to admit their guilt, the Russian Foreign Ministry said.

2012-10-13.title: Opponents and Supporters of Egypt’s President Clash
2012-10-13.abstract: At least 110 people were injured in the bloodiest clashes between the two sides of Egypt’s main ideological divide since the start of the revolt that toppled President Hosni Mubarak.

2012-10-13.title: On Edge as Syria’s War Knocks Ever Harder on the Door to Turkey
2012-10-13.abstract: A journey through the borderlands found that the slow-boiling resentment over the troubles wrought by Syria’s war may be a mere prologue to the danger that lies ahead.

2012-10-13.title: Data on Iran Dims Outlook for Economy
2012-10-13.abstract: Reduced oil production and other factors contributed to a stressed economy likely to exert new pressure on the rial, Iran’s currency, according to the International Energy Agency.

2012-10-13.title: Focus Was on Tripoli in Requests for Security in Libya
2012-10-13.abstract: Calls to extend the tours of security teams that were denied by the State Department largely concerned the embassy in Tripoli — not Benghazi, the site of a lethal attack.

2012-10-14.title: Eduard Volodarsky, Screenwriter Banned by Soviets, Dies at 71
2012-10-14.abstract: Mr. Volodarsky’s films are recognized classics today in Russia, but his scripts cast doubt on the carefully manicured history of the Soviet Union and spent years on the shelves of state studios.

2012-10-14.title: Libya Struggles to Curb Militias, the Only Police
2012-10-14.abstract: The weak Libyan government faces the task of trying to rein in powerful militias while depending on them as the only armed forces, a conundrum that has trapped the country in a state of lawlessness.

2012-10-14.title: Pakistani Girl Shot by Taliban Was Named for a Battlefield Heroine
2012-10-14.abstract: Malala Yousafzai, 14, grievously wounded by Taliban gunmen, remained stable but unconscious on Sunday as her Pakistani doctors debated whether to send her abroad for treatment. Before the attack, as she boldly advocated for girls’ education in Pakistan, she seemed to be living up to her namesake, a 19th-century Afghan heroine.

2012-10-14.title: Red Bull Strong Again; Attendance Lags in Korea
2012-10-14.abstract: It was starting to look like business as usual at the Korean Grand Prix as the Red Bull cars of Mark Webber and Sebastian Vettel finished first and second in qualifying. It also looked like almost no business at the circuit on the southern tip of South Korea as few spectators attended the preliminary sessions.

2012-10-14.title: Newswallah: Bharat Edition
2012-10-14.abstract: A round-up of news from across India.

2012-10-15.title: All-Female Ticket Aims to Be Heard, if Not Seen
2012-10-15.abstract: In the first local elections since 1976 in Hebron, a conservative West Bank city, Maysoun Qawasmi is leading a slate of candidates who she says are better off heard than seen.

2012-10-15.title: North Koreans See Few Gains Below Top Tier
2012-10-15.abstract: Interviews in China with four North Koreans suggest daily life has not improved under Kim Jong-un for those who fall outside the nation’s elite.

2012-10-15.title: Mining Contract Details Disclosed in Afghanistan
2012-10-15.abstract: The West has made greater openness in the Afghan government’s financial dealings a condition of billions of dollars in development assistance and aid money.

2012-10-15.title: Norodom Sihanouk, Cambodian Leader Through Shifting Allegiances, Dies at 89
2012-10-15.abstract: Mr. Sihanouk, who was alternately charming and ruthless, raised the stature of Cambodia and won its independence from French colonial rulers in 1953.

2012-10-15.title: As Tension Escalates, Turkey Issues a Ban on All Syrian Aircraft
2012-10-15.abstract: The announcement followed Syria’s ban on Turkish aircraft and became the latest volley in an increasingly aggressive dispute between the two neighbors over Syria’s devastating civil war.

2012-10-16.title: Doctor’s Bath for Corpses Reinvigorates Cold Cases
2012-10-16.abstract: In the violence-torn border city of Ciudad Juárez, Mexico, a rehydration technique that can restore clues lost to decomposition has won its creator renown.

2012-10-16.title: Afghan Army’s Turnover Threatens U.S. Strategy
2012-10-16.abstract: The Afghan Army is so plagued with desertions and low re-enlistment rates that it has to replace a third of its entire force every year, officials say.

2012-10-16.title: Suicide ‘Insider’ Attack Kills Six in Afghanistan
2012-10-16.abstract: Two Americans are among the dead from a suicide vest detonated by a member of the Afghan intelligence. In a separate episode, Afghan officials say three children were killed in a coalition airstrike.

2012-10-16.title: U.N. Envoy Seeks Pause in Syria for Holiday
2012-10-16.abstract: The envoy, Lakhdar Brahimi, has enlisted Iran’s help in trying to persuade President Bashar al-Assad to support a temporary cease-fire.

2012-10-16.title: Election-Year Stakes Overshadow Nuances of Libya Investigation
2012-10-16.abstract: After a month of conflicting statements and partisan criticism, the circumstances surrounding the deadly attack in Benghazi have become clouded in ambiguities and questions.

2012-10-17.title: Venezuela’s Opposition Struggles for Unity
2012-10-17.abstract: Battered by the re-election of President Hugo Chávez the opposition has the difficult task of rousing its supporters for the elections for governors in the nation’s 23 states on Dec. 16.

2012-10-17.title: Talks on Euro Zone Approach, This Time Under Calmer Conditions
2012-10-17.abstract: For once, the crisis atmosphere that usually accompanies such summit meetings is absent, with general recognition that the euro seems here to stay.

2012-10-17.title: Candidates Spar Sharply Over China
2012-10-17.abstract: Foreign policy issues provoked two of the sharpest moments of the second debate between President Obama and Mitt Romney. They clashed over the attack that killed four Americans in Libya, and there was a testy exchange about trade policy toward China.

2012-10-17.title: C.I.A. Officer Among Dead in Bombing by Afghan
2012-10-17.abstract: An officer for the Central Intelligence Agency was killed on Saturday in a suicide bombing in southern Afghanistan, American officials said Tuesday.

2012-10-17.title: Anger in New Zealand Over Leaky Government Computers
2012-10-17.abstract: New Zealanders were outraged last week when an online journalist was able to use a public computer to gain access to sensitive information from the Ministry of Social Development. He found case files including the names of debtors, children up for adoption and a social services client who had attempted suicide.

2012-10-18.title: Colombia Tries Again to End Drug-Fed War
2012-10-18.abstract: For the first time in a decade, rebels and the government of Colombia came together with the goal of ending the longest-running war in the Western Hemisphere.

2012-10-18.title: War Comes to Damascus
2012-10-18.abstract: In the Syrian capital, the change from just a few months ago is unmistakable.

2012-10-18.title: Questions Raised in Deaths of Afghan Children in Coalition Strike
2012-10-18.abstract: The NATO-led coalition raised the possibility that the Taliban had been using the children to place roadside bombs for them.

2012-10-18.title: Chile: Extradition Request Approved in 1973 Case of Americans Killed
2012-10-18.abstract: The Supreme Court in Chile approved an extradition request on Wednesday against a former American Navy officer indicted in the killings of two United States citizens in the aftermath of the 1973 coup.

2012-10-18.title: Vatican to Send Delegation to Syria
2012-10-18.abstract: The Vatican said Wednesday that it would send a high-profile delegation to Syria, including Cardinal Timothy M. Dolan of New York, to encourage a political solution to the war there.

2012-10-19.title: 5 Seats on Security Council Filled
2012-10-19.abstract: The United Nations General Assembly elected Argentina, Australia, Luxembourg, Rwanda and South Korea on Thursday to two-year terms as nonpermanent members of the 15-seat Security Council.

2012-10-19.title: Killings Derail Effort at Grass-Roots Governance in India
2012-10-19.abstract: The unsolved murders of two officials have set off a panic among the new village councils in a district of Kashmir.

2012-10-19.title: South Korea: Professor Acquitted in Obscenity Case
2012-10-19.abstract: A law professor indicted on charges that he posted a series of photographs showing a man’s genitals on his Internet blog was acquitted on Thursday.

2012-10-19.title: On These Soccer Fields, a Brief Respite from the Killing Fields
2012-10-19.abstract: This year’s Homeless World Cup, in Mexico City, drew young people whose lives have been affected by the particular pain of the country these days: drug violence.

2012-10-19.title: A Respite From Violence
2012-10-19.abstract: At the Homeless World Cup, young people were able to turn to soccer to escape the drug violence plaguing Mexico.

2012-10-20.title: Love-Scorned Sorceress on the Loose
2012-10-20.abstract: If you find the story of Medea — murderess of her own children, incinerator of her rival in love, vengeful troublemaker par excellence — to be too gruesome, you should probably stay away from the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées, currently engaged in performing not just one but a series of three operas about the love-scorned sorceress.

2012-10-20.title: France: Authorities Stopped Tracking Toulouse Gunman Before His Attacks
2012-10-20.abstract: The French police and intelligence services stopped monitoring a Toulouse man who killed several people four months later, according to leaked documents.

2012-10-20.title: North Korea: Threats Over Propaganda
2012-10-20.abstract: North Korea threatened on Friday to attack South Korea if activists proceeded with a plan to send leaflets across the border criticizing the North’s government.

2012-10-20.title: Opening the Gate to a World All His Own
2012-10-20.abstract: William Christie, the founder of a Baroque orchestra, opened his extraordinary garden in Thiré, France, for a music festival this August.

2012-10-20.title: Another Day, Another Claim That Castro Is Really Dead
2012-10-20.abstract: More rumors that Fidel Castro has died are being denied by the government and his family.

2012-10-21.title: Mixed Results for Fatah Amid Low Turnout in Municipal Elections in West Bank
2012-10-21.abstract: The dominant Fatah faction of the Palestinian Authority saw mixed results as just under 55 percent of eligible voters cast ballots, according to election officials.

2012-10-21.title: Maggots in the Pasta: Europe Screens Tainted Chinese Food
2012-10-21.abstract: It has been a rough year for Chinese food exports to Europe. There was arsenic in calamari going to Cyprus and maggots in pasta bound for Italy. At least the norovirus outbreak that sickened 11,000 Germans — most of them schoolchildren — has subsided. The culprit: frozen strawberries.

2012-10-21.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-10-21.abstract: Best of long form journalism this week.

2012-10-21.title: Angry Lebanese Attempt to Storm Government Offices
2012-10-21.abstract: Lebanon’s jittery composure throughout the long Syrian uprising wobbled but held on Sunday, as political and religious leaders quelled street protests.

2012-10-21.title: U.S. and Iran Deny Plan for Nuclear Talks
2012-10-21.abstract: Both the Iranian Foreign Ministry and the White House rejected a report that they had agreed to one-on-one talks on Iran’s nuclear program.

2012-10-22.title: Civic Duty and Economic Worries Drive Palestinian Voters in Parts of West Bank
2012-10-22.abstract: The municipal elections, the first of any kind in the Palestinian territories in six years, were dismissed by many as unimportant because Hamas refused to take part.

2012-10-22.title: In Guatemalan Tourist Haven, Corruption Case Is Talk of the Town
2012-10-22.abstract: In Guatemala, where corruption is ingrained, a case against the mayor of Antigua is being viewed by many as a major step toward tackling the kind of political malfeasance long taken for granted.

2012-10-22.title: Libyan Town Under Siege Is a Center of Resistance to the New Government
2012-10-22.abstract: A one-time bastion of support for Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi, Bani Walid is under assault, stirring protests elsewhere and imperiling the country’s fragile political transition.

2012-10-22.title: Many Urge Next Leader of China to Liberalize
2012-10-22.abstract: As the nation’s critical leadership transition approaches, officials, policy advisers and intellectuals are again pushing for what they broadly call “reform.”

2012-10-22.title: Exhibit A in Debate Over Castro’s State
2012-10-22.abstract: With rumors churning that Fidel Castro was dead or nearly so, a former vice president of Venezuela showed off a picture of what he said was his recent encounter with the former Cuban leader.

2012-10-23.title: Stung by Criticism, Russian Lawmakers Point to Human Rights Abuses in U.S.
2012-10-23.abstract: At an old-fashioned hearing in the Russian Parliament, a discussion of human rights violations committed by the United States was reminiscent of the cold war era.

2012-10-23.title: China: Leftists Express Support for Disgraced Politician
2012-10-23.abstract: Dozens of leftists signed an open letter to the chairman of the Standing Committee of the National People’s Congress, asking him not to expel Bo Xilai from the congress.

2012-10-23.title: Gaza: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 2 Palestinian Militants
2012-10-23.abstract: Two Palestinian militants were killed in separate Israeli airstrikes in northern Gaza on Monday.

2012-10-23.title: China: A Tibetan Protester Sets Himself on Fire
2012-10-23.abstract: A Tibetan man, Dhondup, is believed to have died after he set himself on fire on Monday to protest Chinese rule.

2012-10-23.title: Cuba: Castro Says He’s Fine and Pokes Fun at Rumors
2012-10-23.abstract: Fidel Castro, rumored to have suffered a debilitating stroke or perhaps to have died, sent a message to the world on Monday: “I don’t even remember what a headache feels like.”

2012-10-24.title: 4 Children Killed in Afghanistan Firefight
2012-10-24.abstract: Because bullets were flying from both sides during the hourlong battle, international forces stopped short of taking responsibility for the children’s deaths, which occurred on Sunday.

2012-10-24.title: Report Faults French Police Over Killings
2012-10-24.abstract: A highly critical report detailed failures by French security and intelligence services regarding Mohammed Merah, the self-proclaimed jihadist who killed seven people in March.

2012-10-24.title: The Whiff of Conflict Grows in Mali
2012-10-24.abstract: Despite an international consensus that military force is required to take back large areas of northern Mali controlled by Islamist rebels, there are few volunteers.

2012-10-24.title: Egyptian Court Declines to Rule on the Legality of Drafting a New Constitution
2012-10-24.abstract: A ruling on dissolving the predominately Islamist constitutional committee was deferred to a higher authority, a decision that could allow time for the work to be completed.

2012-10-24.title: Jailed Russian Opposition Leader was Abused, Groups Say
2012-10-24.abstract: Leonid Razvozzhayev was held in a basement and denied food and water for more than two days, human rights officials said.

2012-10-25.title: Amid Cutbacks, Greek Doctors Offer Message to Poor: You Are Not Alone
2012-10-25.abstract: Until recently, Greece had a typical European health system offering universal care, but the unemployed are now often left on their own if they get sick.

2012-10-25.title: Europe’s Top Health Official Quits, and the Bloc Has a Mystery on Its Hands
2012-10-25.abstract: A top-level European Union resignation, Big Tobacco, burglaries and talk of cash payments have made for a scandal.

2012-10-25.title: Bleary-Eyed Troops Fight a Building at a Time in Syria
2012-10-25.abstract: Syrian soldiers’ battle to retake a rebel-held school in Homs is a war of attrition, in which the opposition gains ground and the government takes it back.

2012-10-25.title: Wilhelm Brasse Dies at 94; Documented Nazis’ Victims
2012-10-25.abstract: Mr. Brasse was a photographer and prisoner at Auschwitz who was haunted by the pictures he was forced to take.

2012-10-25.title: British Parliament Questions Former BBC Chief’s Role
2012-10-25.abstract: As the investigation into the BBC’s handling of Jimmy Savile continues, the actions of its former director general, Mark Thompson, are coming under increased scrutiny.

2012-10-26.title: Four International Service Members Killed in Afghan Attacks
2012-10-26.abstract: In what are being investigated as possible insider attacks, two British services members died in a gun battle and two United States Forces-Afghanistan members were killed.

2012-10-26.title: Netanyahu Announces Coalition With a Nationalist Party
2012-10-26.abstract: The move sharpened the divide between the left and right camps in Israeli politics, after years during which the major-party leaders had gravitated toward the political center.

2012-10-26.title: Israeli Officials Asked to Be Silent on Issue of U.S.-Iran Talks
2012-10-26.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his aides were trying to head off political problems over a report on the possibility of bilateral talks between the United States and Iran.

2012-10-26.title: Iran Said to Nearly Finish Nuclear Enrichment Plant
2012-10-26.abstract: The installation of the last of nearly 3,000 centrifuges, deep under a mountain inside a military base, puts Iran closer to being able to build a nuclear weapon.

2012-10-26.title: Panetta Says Risk Impeded Deployment to Benghazi
2012-10-26.abstract: Defense Secretary Leon E. Panetta says that he and top military commanders felt that they needed more information before deploying troops after the Sept. 11 attack.

2012-10-27.title: Culinary Battles of Mitterrand’s Chef Go From Kitchen to Screen
2012-10-27.abstract: The story of  Danièle Delpeuch, a self-trained cook, and President François Mitterrand  has been celebrated in a fiction-frosted film released last month.

2012-10-27.title: A Journey Through Nepal’s Mustang Region
2012-10-27.abstract: Beijing correspondent Edward Wong takes readers on a Himalayan trek through Nepal’s other-worldly Mustang region where the line between myth and history blurs.

2012-10-27.title: China Blocks and Criticizes Investigation Into Premier
2012-10-27.abstract: The Chinese government immediately blocked access to the English and Chinese-language Web sites of The New York Times in response to an article on the assets accumulated by the family of Prime Minister Wen Jiabao.

2012-10-27.title: England: Belarussian Is Granted Asylum
2012-10-27.abstract: Andrei Sannikov, a prominent dissident from Belarus and former presidential candidate, has been granted political asylum in Britain, his wife said Friday.

2012-10-27.title: China: Two Tibetan Self-Immolations
2012-10-27.abstract: Lhamo Tseten, aged 24, burned himself to death on Friday. It was the fourth self-immolation this week in an area that Tibetans call Sangchu County.

2012-10-28.title: A Village Rape Shatters a Family, and India’s Traditional Silence
2012-10-28.abstract: An outcry over a string of recent rapes has focused national attention on India’s rising number of sexual assaults and exposed the male-dominated power structure in Haryana.

2012-10-28.title: Chinese Premier’s Family Disputes Article on Riches
2012-10-28.abstract: The statement about a New York Times article marks a rare instance of a powerful Chinese political family responding directly to a foreign media report.

2012-10-28.title: Chinese Censors Work to Quash Story on Vast Wealth of Prime Minister’s Relatives
2012-10-28.abstract: Lawyers representing the family of Wen Jiabao, the Chinese prime minister, have assailed a story in The New York Times that documents the vast wealth of some of Mr. Wen’s close relatives. Chinese censors also continued to erase online messages about the story while blocking search terms like “Diamond Queen” and “2.7 billion.”

2012-10-28.title: In Cabinet Reshuffle, a Focus on Youth
2012-10-28.abstract: Changes include promotion of many junior ministers, but not Rahul Gandhi.

2012-10-28.title: Plot on U.S. Targets Cited in 11 Arrests by Indonesia
2012-10-28.abstract: The suspects, said to be part of a relatively new militant group, were planning attacks on several high-profile targets in the country, including the U.S. Embassy in Jakarta.

2012-10-29.title: Governing Party Claims Victory in Ukraine Elections
2012-10-29.abstract: The governing party of President Viktor F. Yanukovich declared victory in Ukraine on Sunday, but preliminary exit polls showed opposition parties making strong gains.

2012-10-29.title: Corruption Probes in China Said to Rise 13 Percent
2012-10-29.abstract: China has become increasingly vigilant about ferreting out official corruption and bribe-taking, according to a new report in the state-run media. Investigations are up, and even the spouses of officials are being lectured about how to avoid corruption.

2012-10-29.title: Presidential Appointments to Keep
2012-10-29.abstract: Some significant presidential appointments are looming, regardless of who wins the election on Nov. 6. New secretaries of state and the Treasury will have to be selected, and later, a new chairman of the Federal Reserve to succeed Ben Bernanke.

2012-10-29.title: At Bangalore’s Gated Enclaves, the Chaos Outside Comes Knocking at the Door
2012-10-29.abstract: Trash, water and security problems loom for the city’s upper class.

2012-10-29.title: A Conversation With: Asha Bhat, Kashmir Panchayat Leader
2012-10-29.abstract: Local government officials are slow and corrupt, making village development difficult, she says.

2012-10-30.title: Yet Another Blow to Haiti From a Natural Disaster
2012-10-30.abstract: At least 52 people have died from the impact of the storm, which has followed the devastating 2010 earthquake, a cholera outbreak and Tropical Storm Isaac.

2012-10-30.title: A Closer Look at the American ‘Pivot’
2012-10-30.abstract: A deft new documentary, “The Pivot,” explores and explains the enhanced U.S. emphasis on the Asia-Pacific region. American diplomats say the strategy is not about “containing” China, but that’s not the prevailing view on the mainland.

2012-10-30.title: Is India’s Rising Billionaire Wealth Bad for the Country?
2012-10-30.abstract: Inequality, measured by wealth controlled by the richest, is higher in India than in China or Brazil.

2012-10-30.title: A Dive Into Our Archives: Buchwald Meets Cousteau
2012-10-30.abstract: The year: 1956. The subject: Prince Rainier’s approaching wedding to Grace Kelly. The quote: “As you know, a woman wants to be the sole interest of her husband,” said Jacques-Yves Cousteau to Art Buchwald. “She is jealous of his hobbies.” The hobby in question? Read on.

2012-10-30.title: IHT Quick Read: Tuesday, Oct. 30
2012-10-30.abstract: News, arts, fashion and sports in today’s International Herald Tribune.

2012-10-31.title: Israeli Defense Chief Says Iran Postponed Nuclear Ambitions
2012-10-31.abstract: Defense Minister Ehud Barak used Iran’s conversion of some enriched uranium into fuel rods for civilian power generation as evidence that making weapons had taken a back seat.

2012-10-31.title: Immigrants Have Helped Set Catalonia Apart in Spain
2012-10-31.abstract: Up to 1.5 million immigrants in Catalonia cannot vote in what could be an unofficial referendum on independence, but their  economic contributions may indirectly reinforce the separatists’ case.

2012-10-31.title: Outcry in Japan Over Diversion of Post-Disaster Aid Funds
2012-10-31.abstract: Independent audits cast a harsh light on the bureaucratic morass slowing the reconstruction effort after last year’s earthquake and tsunami.

2012-10-31.title: Clinton Urges Bosnia’s Leaders to Work Together
2012-10-31.abstract: The secretary of state’s comments came as she made a trip through the Balkan region with Catherine Ashton, the European Union’s high representative for foreign policy.

2012-10-31.title: Syrian Air Force Commander Is Reported Killed
2012-10-31.abstract: The government announced that Gen. Abdullah Mahmud al-Khalidi was killed by “terrorists,” and a rebel coalition claimed responsibility.
